close_reason: Added pkcs11_err() helper, replaced 8 boilerplate Backend error constructions
description:
Location: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:178-185 (unsupported EC curve), 277-282 (unsupported key type), 286-289 (CKA_KEY_TYPE missing), 341-344 (CKA_VALUE missing), 549-553 (CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO missing), 507-510 (unsupported signing algorithm), 109-115 (slot index OOB).

Pattern:
    SecretError::Backend {
        backend: "pkcs11",
        source: format!("...").into(),
    }

What this loses: the structured information (slot index, curve OID, key type kind, attribute type) is concatenated into a String and lost from the type system. Callers cannot match on "this was a missing-attribute error" vs "this was an unsupported-curve error" without parsing the string.

What idiomatic Rust would do: define a Pkcs11Error enum locally with structured variants:

#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
enum Pkcs11Error {
    #[error("slot index {index} not found ({available} slot(s) with a token)")]
    SlotIndexOutOfRange { index: usize, available: usize },
    #[error("unsupported EC curve (CKA_EC_PARAMS = {0:02x?}); only P-256 is supported")]
    UnsupportedEcCurve(Vec<u8>),
    #[error("unsupported key type: {0}")]
    UnsupportedKeyType(KeyType),
    #[error("required attribute {0} missing on object")]
    MissingAttribute(&'static str),
    ...
}

Then SecretError::Backend wraps Pkcs11Error: source: pkcs11_err.into(). Downstream code can downcast() to Pkcs11Error if it wants to handle specific cases.

Why it matters here, not just style: this crate is a building block for higher-level secretx operations and FIPS-audited deployments. Auditors and integrators want to map "key type X is unsupported" to a deployment decision, not parse error strings. The structured error is also more searchable in observability tooling.

Not strictly required — string-formatted errors are fine in many crates — but for a security backend this is worth the small refactor.
title: Internal errors stringly-formatted instead of structured Pkcs11Error enum
